career.description: Leads a 35-person cross-functional engineering and research team building Google's next-generation autonomous AI agent platform (Project Mariner & Gemini Agents). Responsible for the full technical roadmap, product strategy, and safety architecture of agentic systems deployed across Google Search, Workspace, and Android. Defining the multi-agent orchestration standards now adopted across all Alphabet AI products.
key_achievements: Architected multi-agent orchestration framework serving 2B+ daily interactions globally
Reduced agent hallucination rate by 67% via novel self-verification architecture
Launched Gemini Agents to 190 countries with 99.99% uptime SLA
Filed 4 patents on agentic reasoning and memory architectures
Built and scaled the Agentic AI team from 8 to 35 engineers & researchers

career.description: Core contributor to LaMDA (now Bard/Gemini) and early PaLM language model research teams. Led the reasoning and factual grounding sub-team. Pioneered chain-of-thought prompting methodology that became the industry standard for eliciting reasoning from LLMs. Published 14 papers during this period at top-tier venues.
key_achievements: Co-authored 'Chain-of-Thought Prompting' paper — 4,100+ citations (NeurIPS 2022)
Led 12-person research team on LaMDA factual grounding module
Improved PaLM reasoning benchmarks (GSM8K) from 58% to 74% accuracy
Received Google Research Outstanding Contribution Award 2020 and 2021
Published 14 papers at top-tier venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR)

career.description: Co-founded an enterprise AI automation startup focused on no-code agentic workflow automation for Fortune 500 enterprises. Built and led a 28-person engineering team across SF, Bangalore, and London. Grew to 400+ enterprise clients including Microsoft, Salesforce, and JPMorgan.
key_achievements: Raised $12M Series A led by Sequoia Capital (valuation: $60M)
Acquired by Google for $180M — 3x return for investors in 18 months
400+ enterprise clients at time of acquisition
$8M ARR at time of acquisition
Built engineering team of 28 across San Francisco, Bangalore, and London

career.description: Doctoral research residency focused on Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) and AI alignment techniques. Worked directly with Ilya Sutskever and Dario Amodei on early GPT alignment strategies. Co-authored a landmark alignment paper with 3,200+ citations.
key_achievements: Co-authored foundational RLHF alignment paper — 3,200+ citations
Developed reward modeling framework adopted in GPT-3 fine-tuning pipeline
Promoted from intern to Research Resident within 3 months
Worked directly with Ilya Sutskever and Dario Amodei

career.reports_to: Prof. Daniela Rus (MIT), Prof. Leslie Valiant (Harvard)
career.description: Conducted foundational research in autonomous systems and reinforcement learning under NSF Fellowship. Teaching Fellow for CS 182 (Deep Learning) at Harvard and 6.867 (Machine Learning) at MIT. Mentored 18 undergraduate and graduate students; 6 of whom are now faculty at top universities.
key_achievements: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship recipient
Teaching Fellow for CS 182 (Deep Learning) at Harvard
Teaching Fellow for 6.867 (Machine Learning) at MIT
Mentored 18 undergraduate and graduate students
6 mentees are now faculty at top universities
Published 11 papers during PhD
